docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Watson is a simple terminal based time tracking application highly inspired by td-watson.

See the Get started page to get started with watson.

docs.title: add
docs.text:
watson add [project] [tag1] [tag2] ... [options]

Add a new frame for the specified project and tag(s) at the specified time*.

If no time is specified, the current time is used.*

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description Example -f, --from The start time of the frame. --from "2025-01-01 14:45" -t, --to The end time of the frame. --to "2025-01-01 14:45"

docs.title: Example
docs.text: watson add cookin pizza hawaiian --from "2025-01-01 14:45"

docs.title: cancel
docs.text:
watson cancel

Cancel the currently running frame.

docs.title: edit
docs.text:
watson edit [project] [tag1] [tag2] ... [options]

Edit an existing frame*.

If frame ID is specified, the currently running frame, if any, is used.*

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description Example -f, --from The start time of the frame. --from "2025-01-01 14:45" -i, --id The ID of the frame to edit. --id 02af5e

docs.title: Example
docs.text: watson edit watching-tv avengers --id 02af5e --from "2025-01-01 14:45"

docs.title: log
docs.text:
watson log [options]

Display the frames within the specified time range and meeting the specified criteria.

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description Example -f, --from The start time of the frame. --from "2025-01-01 14:45" -t, --to The end time of the frame. --to "2025-01-01 14:45" -p, --project Show only frames of those projects. --project cooking -t, --tag Show only the frames having those tags. --tag pizza -a, --all Show all frames. --all -d, --day Show the frames of today. --day -w, --week Show the frames of this week. --week -m, --month Show the frames of this month. --month -y, --year Show the frames of this year. --year -r, --reverse Show the frames in reverse order. --reverse -i, --ignore-project Ignore frames of those projects. --ignore-project cooking -g, --ignore-tag Ignore frames having those tags. --ignore-tag pizza

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
watson log -d
watson log --from "2025-01-01 14:45" --to "2025-01-01 14:45"
watson log --from 14:15
watson log -w --project cooking --ignore-tag pizza

docs.title: remove
docs.text:
watson remove [id]

Remove the frame with the specified ID.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: watson remove 02af5e

docs.title: restart
docs.text:
watson restart [id (optional)]

Restart the specified frame with the same project and tags. If no ID is specified, the latest stopped frame is restarted, if any.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
watson restart
watson restart 02af5e

docs.title: start
docs.text:
watson start [project] [tag1] [tag2] ...

Start a new frame for the specified project and tag(s).

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
watson start watching-tv
watson start cooking pizza hawaiian

docs.title: status
docs.text:
watson status

Display the status of the currently running frame.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: watson status

docs.title: stop
docs.text:
watson stop [options]

Stop the currently running frame.

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description Example -a, --at Stop the frame at the specified time. --at "2025-01-01 14:45"

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
watson stop
watson stop --at "2025-01-01 14:45"

docs.title: config
docs.text:
watson config [action] [key] [value]

Get or set a settings value.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
watson config get workTime.endTime
watson config set workTime.endTime 9:15

docs.title: workhours
docs.text:
watson workhours [start|end|reset] [time]

Get or set custom work hours for the current day. If reset is passed, the work hours are reset to the default values.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
watson workhours start 9:15
watson workhours end 17:15
watson workhours reset

docs.title: stats (Work in progress)
docs.text:
watson stats [type] [options]

Display various statistics.

Types possible :

  • projects

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description Example -f, --from The start time of the frame. --from "2025-01-01 14:45" -t, --to The end time of the frame. --to "2025-01-01 14:45" -p, --project Show only frames of those projects. --project cooking -t, --tag Show only the frames having those tags. --tag pizza -a, --all Show all frames. --all -d, --day Show the frames of today. --day -w, --week Show the frames of this week. --week -m, --month Show the frames of this month. --month -y, --year Show the frames of this year. --year -r, --reverse Show the frames in reverse order. --reverse -i, --ignore-project Ignore frames of those projects. --ignore-project cooking -g, --ignore-tag Ignore frames having those tags. --ignore-tag pizza

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: watson stats projects -d

docs.title: todo
docs.text:
watson todo [action] [arguments] [options]

Perform actions on todos, such as

  • Create a todo
  • Remove a todo
  • List todos
  • Edit a todo
  • Complete / uncomplete a todo

docs.title: Actions
docs.text: Action Description add Create a todo remove Remove a todo list List todos edit Edit a todo complete, done Complete a todo uncomplete, undone, undo, reset Uncomplete a todo

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description Example -d, --due The due date of the todo. --due "2025-01-01" -p, --priority The priority of the todo. --priority 1

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
watson todo add "Cook that tasty pizza I saw on YouTube" cooking pizza --due "2026-01-01 12:00" --priority 1
watson todo remove 02af5e
watson todo list
watson todo edit 02af5e "Cook that crazy pizza I saw on YouTube" cooking pizza --due "2026-01-01 12:45" --priority 2
watson todo complete 02af5e
waton todo reset 02af5e

docs.title: project
docs.text:
watson project [action] [arguments] [options]

Perform actions on projects, such as

  • Create a project
  • Remove a project
  • List projects
  • Rename a project

docs.title: Actions
docs.text: Action Description add Create a project remove Remove a project list List projects rename Rename a project

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
watson project add cooking
watson project remove 82af5e
watson project list
watson project rename 82af5e eating

docs.title: tag
docs.text:
watson tag [action] [arguments] [options]

Perform actions on tags, such as

  • Create a tag
  • Remove a tag
  • List tags
  • Rename a tag

docs.title: Actions
docs.text: Action Description add Create a tag remove Remove a tag list List tags rename Rename a tag

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
watson tag add pizza
watson tag remove 02af5e
watson tag list
watson tag rename 02af5e burger

docs.title: What is a frame?
docs.text:
It's a task or an activity. Basically what you're working on. Usually it's a simple name, like cooking or 'project-abc'. You can also add as many tags as you want to describe the frame.

For example, if you're cooking a pizza, you could say watson start cooking pizza. The project is cooking the tag is pizza.

Tags can help you find frames related to specific tasks within a project.

docs.title: Is the application using 12h or 24h time?
docs.text: The application uses 24h time. It's much easier to work with and remove the ambiguity of 12h time.

docs.title: Do I need to create a special project for lunchtime?
docs.text: No, you can specify your lunch time in the settings of the application. By default, it's set to 12:00 to 13:00.

docs.title: Where are the application data stored?
docs.text:
All the data of the application is stored in a folder called .watson in your home directory.

  • On Windows, it's C:\Users\<username>\.watson
  • On Linux and macOS, it's ~/.watson
  • On macOS, it's /Users/<username>/.watson

The directory contains several elements :

  • The logs directory contains the logs of the application
  • The settings.json file contains the settings of the application (which you can also edit through the application)
  • The database file data.db which contains the frames, projects, tags, etc.

docs.title: How does time input works?
docs.text:
There are several parsing rules available to speed up the time input process, so you don't have to type the date time. Of course, you can always type the full date time manually if you want to, but here are some shortcuts to save up some time.

  • 1-2 digits means hour. For example
    • 3 means 03:00
    • 13 means 13:00
    • 88 means 08:08
  • 3-4 digits means hour:minute. For example
    • 1234 means 12:34
    • 845 means 08:45
    • 138 means 13:08
  • 2 sets of digits means day hour. For example
    • 13 14 means 13th of the current month at 14:00
    • 3 1245 means 3rd of the current month at 12:45
  • hyphen separated digits means year-month-day. For example
    • 3-13 means March 13th of the current year
    • 10-23 852 means October 23rd of the current year at 08:52
    • 2023-4-13 14 means April 13th of 2023 at 14:00

docs.title: How does a frame start time and end time work?
docs.text:
A frame is actually simply a point in time. It only stores the time at which it was created. That means that the end time of a frame is technically the start time of "next thing" that comes after it. This design removes the tedious task of having to perfectly mix and match the start and end time of tasks.

For example, you're working on something, then your boss calls you for something else at 9:13 for 2 minutes, then you go back on your project, but then a coworker calls you for something 4 minutes later. After all of that, you realized you forgot to add your tasks in the application. If you'd need to manage end times, that will probably look like this :

  • Add a task "call with the boss" from 9:13 to 9:15
  • Add a task "work on project" from 9:15 to 9:20
  • Add a task "call with the coworker" from 9:20 to 9:24
  • Add a task "work on project" from 9:24 to now

Kind of tedious, isn't it?

Now, what if you only need to remember the start time, it's much easier :

  • Add a task "call with the boss" at 9:13
  • Add a task "work on project" at 9:15
  • Add a task "call with the coworker" at 9:20
  • Add a task "work on project" at 9:24

And all frames are gonna adjust automatically, no more "when did it end? how long was the call? etc."!

But, how does it knows when the day ends? Well, there are settings for that. You can set your work hours in the settings of the application. By default, it's set to 8:00 to 16:00.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: • .NET 8.0 or higher

docs.title: Using dotnet (easiest)
docs.text: dotnet tool install --global nomis51.watson

docs.title: Standalone
docs.text:
• Go to the Release page
  • Download the latest zip file for your platform
  • Extract
  • Run Watson.exe

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
When you want to track a task or an activity, you create a new frame with the following command :

watson start cooking pizza

Each frame need a project. You can also add as many tags as you want to describe the frame. In the example above, we have the project cooking with the tag pizza. So we're basically "cooking a pizza" in that frame.

When you're ready for another task or activity, you can start another frame. You can also stop the current frame at any time.

You can display the status of the current frame with the status command.

And you can also list of all the frames within a time period with the log command.

For more details about all the available commands and options run watson help or visit the commands page.
